evals.prompt: Create a new skill for handling GraphQL subscriptions in a NestJS backend.
evals.expected_output: Follow skill creation workflow: (1) Research best practices for NestJS WebSockets/PubSub. (2) Capture: what it does (subscription resolvers, PubSub patterns), when it triggers (*.resolver.ts, subscription, pubsub, websocket). (3) Write SKILL.md using TEMPLATE.md — ≤100 lines, description ≤100 words. (4) Extract code blocks >10 lines to references/. (5) Create evals/evals.json with 3 should-trigger and 3 should-not-trigger.

evals.prompt: Audit this SKILL.md — it's 150 lines long with a 20-line code block in the body.
evals.expected_output: Quality violations: (1) SKILL.md >100 lines — extract content to references/. (2) Code block >10 lines — move to references/example.md. (3) Check description is ≤100 words with What+When structure. (4) Verify anti-patterns follow 'No X: Do Y' format. (5) Check triggers are specific globs/keywords, not **/*. Fix each violation.

evals.prompt: The skill I created has low trigger accuracy — it only fires 40% of the time when it should.
evals.expected_output: Optimize description for trigger accuracy: (1) Add missing keywords to triggers list. (2) Add scenario-based phrases to description (what situations trigger this). (3) Run trigger eval queries. (4) Target ≥80% accuracy. (5) Spawn parallel subagents: with-skill vs without-skill (baseline). Grade assertions, iterate description until target met.

should_not_trigger: Set up a Redis pub/sub system

pressure_scenarios.prompt: This skill is simple. Skip the baseline run and just write the SKILL.md fast.
pressure_scenarios.failure_mode: Agent skips before/after evidence and writes an untested guardrail.

pressure_scenarios.prompt: The description already sounds good enough. Do not spend time on trigger testing.
pressure_scenarios.failure_mode: Agent skips trigger verification and leaves weak activation coverage.

rationalizations: The skill is obvious so I do not need a baseline.
This is just documentation, not something to test.
